Files

nauka_org/project/env/
store.rs

1//! Environment persistence on the SurrealDB-backed cluster store.
2//!
3//! P2.11 (sifrah/nauka#215) migrated this module from the legacy raw-KV
4//! cluster path to the native SurrealDB SDK on top of [`EmbeddedDb`],
5//! following the same pattern that P2.9 / P2.10 established for
6//! [`crate::store::OrgStore`] and
7//! [`crate::project::store::ProjectStore`]. Every method now reaches
8//! the SDK via `self.db.client()` and writes/reads against the
9//! SCHEMAFULL `env` table defined in `layers/org/schemas/env.surql`
10//! (applied to the cluster by `nauka_state::apply_cluster_schemas` at
11//! bootstrap per ADR 0004).
12//!
13//! The store holds an [`EmbeddedDb`] directly, same as the other two
14//! org-layer stores. Call sites pass `cluster_db.embedded().clone()`
15//! at construction time; the clone is cheap (`Arc`-shared
16//! `Surreal<Db>` router) and lets the store share the underlying
17//! connection with the rest of the cluster path.
18//!
19//! The legacy `env` / `env-idx` / `_reg_v2` sidecar namespaces are
20//! gone: the schema's composite unique index on `(project, name)` is
21//! now the source of truth for "have we seen this (project, name)
22//! pair before?", and `SELECT * FROM env` walks every row directly
23//! without a separate key list.
24//!
25//! The `Environment` → SurrealDB JSON bridge is hand-written rather
26//! than `SurrealValue`-derived for the same reason the sibling stores
27//! bridge manually: `Environment` embeds [`ResourceMeta`] which carries
28//! an `id: String` field that collides with SurrealDB's reserved
29//! record-id slot. We bind each column explicitly in the
30//! `CREATE … SET …` form so the offending `id` field is dropped on the
31//! way in, and we cast the `created_at` / `updated_at` Unix-epoch
32//! values through `<datetime>$iso8601_string` to match the schema's
33//! `TYPE datetime` constraint.
34
35use nauka_core::id::{EnvId, OrgId, ProjectId};
36use nauka_core::resource::epoch_to_iso8601;
37use nauka_core::resource::ResourceMeta;
38use nauka_state::sdk_bridge::{classify_create_error, iso8601_to_epoch, thing_to_id_string};
39use nauka_state::EmbeddedDb;
40use serde::Deserialize;
41
42use crate::project;
43
44use super::types::Environment;
45
46/// SurrealDB table backing this store. Defined by
47/// `layers/org/schemas/env.surql` as `DEFINE TABLE env SCHEMAFULL`
48/// and applied at bootstrap via `nauka_state::apply_cluster_schemas`.
49const ENV_TABLE: &str = "env";
50
51pub struct EnvStore {
52    db: EmbeddedDb,
53}
54
55impl EnvStore {
56    /// Build an [`EnvStore`] over a SurrealDB handle.
57    ///
58    /// Call sites that already hold a cluster-DB wrapper pass
59    /// `cluster_db.embedded().clone()` — the [`EmbeddedDb`] is cheap
60    /// to clone (`Arc`-shared internally), so constructing per-request
61    /// stores off the same cluster handle is free.
62    pub fn new(db: EmbeddedDb) -> Self {
63        Self { db }
64    }
65
66    /// Create a new environment within a project.
67    ///
68    /// Resolves the owning project by its human-readable name (scoped
69    /// to `org_name`), builds an `Environment` with a fresh [`EnvId`],
70    /// and writes it to the `env` table via
71    /// `CREATE type::record($tbl, $id) SET …`. The schema's composite
72    /// unique index on `(project, name)` rejects duplicate
73    /// `(project, name)` pairs at the database level, closing the old
74    /// "check then insert" race.
75    ///
76    /// Duplicate-name conflicts surface as a flat `anyhow::Error` with
77    /// a human-friendly `"environment '<name>' already exists in
78    /// project '<project>'"` message so the CLI keeps producing the
79    /// same wording it did before P2.11.
80    pub async fn create(
81        &self,
82        name: &str,
83        project_name: &str,
84        org_name: &str,
85    ) -> anyhow::Result<Environment> {
86        // Resolve the owning project via the post-P2.10 store that
87        // takes an `EmbeddedDb` directly.
88        let proj_store = project::store::ProjectStore::new(self.db.clone());
89        let project = proj_store
90            .get(project_name, Some(org_name))
91            .await?
92            .ok_or_else(|| {
93                anyhow::anyhow!("project '{project_name}' not found in org '{org_name}'")
94            })?;
95
96        let env = Environment {
97            meta: ResourceMeta::new(EnvId::generate().to_string(), name),
98            project_id: project.meta.id.clone().into(),
99            project_name: project.meta.name.clone(),
100            org_id: project.org_id.clone(),
101            org_name: project.org_name.clone(),
102        };
103
104        let created_at_iso = epoch_to_iso8601(env.meta.created_at);
105        let updated_at_iso = epoch_to_iso8601(env.meta.updated_at);
106        let labels_json = serde_json::to_value(&env.meta.labels)
107            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("serialise labels: {e}"))?;
108
109        let query_result = self
110            .db
111            .client()
112            .query(
113                "CREATE type::record($tbl, $id) SET \
114                 name = $name, \
115                 status = $status, \
116                 labels = $labels, \
117                 project = $project, \
118                 project_name = $project_name, \
119                 org = $org, \
120                 org_name = $org_name, \
121                 created_at = <datetime>$created_at, \
122                 updated_at = <datetime>$updated_at",
123            )
124            .bind(("tbl", ENV_TABLE))
125            .bind(("id", env.meta.id.clone()))
126            .bind(("name", env.meta.name.clone()))
127            .bind(("status", env.meta.status.clone()))
128            .bind(("labels", labels_json))
129            .bind(("project", env.project_id.as_str().to_string()))
130            .bind(("project_name", env.project_name.clone()))
131            .bind(("org", env.org_id.as_str().to_string()))
132            .bind(("org_name", env.org_name.clone()))
133            .bind(("created_at", created_at_iso))
134            .bind(("updated_at", updated_at_iso))
135            .await;
136        let response = match query_result {
137            Ok(r) => r,
138            Err(e) => {
139                return Err(classify_create_error_with_project(
140                    name,
141                    project_name,
142                    &e.to_string(),
143                ))
144            }
145        };
146        if let Err(e) = response.check() {
147            return Err(classify_create_error_with_project(
148                name,
149                project_name,
150                &e.to_string(),
151            ));
152        }
153
154        Ok(env)
155    }
156
157    /// Look up an environment by id (when the input looks like an
158    /// [`EnvId`]) or by human name (otherwise).
159    ///
160    /// The id path is a direct record-id `SELECT`; the name path
161    /// requires both `project_name` and `org_name` so the lookup can
162    /// resolve the parent project first and then use the composite
163    /// `(project, name)` index. Both paths return `Ok(None)` when no
164    /// row matches.
165    pub async fn get(
166        &self,
167        name_or_id: &str,
168        project_name: Option<&str>,
169        org_name: Option<&str>,
170    ) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Environment>> {
171        if EnvId::looks_like_id(name_or_id) {
172            return self.get_by_id(name_or_id).await;
173        }
174
175        let project_name = project_name
176            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("--project required to resolve environment by name"))?;
177
178        // Resolve the owning project so we can query by its record id
179        // via the composite `(project, name)` unique index.
180        let proj_store = project::store::ProjectStore::new(self.db.clone());
181        let project = proj_store
182            .get(project_name, org_name)
183            .await?
184            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("project '{project_name}' not found"))?;
185
186        self.get_by_project_and_name(&project.meta.id, name_or_id)
187            .await
188    }
189
190    async fn get_by_id(&self, id: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Environment>> {
191        let mut response = self
192            .db
193            .client()
194            .query("SELECT * FROM type::record($tbl, $id)")
195            .bind(("tbl", ENV_TABLE))
196            .bind(("id", id.to_string()))
197            .await
198            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
199        let raw: Vec<serde_json::Value> = response.take(0).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
200        decode_first(raw)
201    }
202
203    async fn get_by_project_and_name(
204        &self,
205        project_id: &str,
206        name: &str,
207    ) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Environment>> {
208        let mut response = self
209            .db
210            .client()
211            .query("SELECT * FROM env WHERE project = $project AND name = $name LIMIT 1")
212            .bind(("project", project_id.to_string()))
213            .bind(("name", name.to_string()))
214            .await
215            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
216        let raw: Vec<serde_json::Value> = response.take(0).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
217        decode_first(raw)
218    }
219
220    /// List every environment in the cluster, optionally filtered by
221    /// the owning project and/or org (by human name or by id).
222    pub async fn list(
223        &self,
224        project_name: Option<&str>,
225        org_name: Option<&str>,
226    ) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<Environment>> {
227        let mut response = self
228            .db
229            .client()
230            .query("SELECT * FROM env")
231            .await
232            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
233        let raw: Vec<serde_json::Value> = response.take(0).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
234        let envs: Vec<Environment> = raw
235            .into_iter()
236            .filter_map(|v| {
237                serde_json::from_value::<EnvRow>(v)
238                    .ok()
239                    .map(EnvRow::into_environment)
240            })
241            .collect();
242
243        match (project_name, org_name) {
244            (Some(proj), Some(org)) => Ok(envs
245                .into_iter()
246                .filter(|e| {
247                    (e.project_name == proj || e.project_id.as_str() == proj)
248                        && (e.org_name == org || e.org_id.as_str() == org)
249                })
250                .collect()),
251            (Some(proj), None) => Ok(envs
252                .into_iter()
253                .filter(|e| e.project_name == proj || e.project_id.as_str() == proj)
254                .collect()),
255            (None, Some(org)) => Ok(envs
256                .into_iter()
257                .filter(|e| e.org_name == org || e.org_id.as_str() == org)
258                .collect()),
259            (None, None) => Ok(envs),
260        }
261    }
262
263    /// Delete an environment by name or id.
264    ///
265    /// `env` is the bottom of the org → project → env hierarchy, so
266    /// there is no child-count check here — once the env is gone, the
267    /// parent project can be deleted whenever the operator wants.
268    pub async fn delete(
269        &self,
270        name_or_id: &str,
271        project_name: &str,
272        org_name: &str,
273    ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
274        let env = self
275            .get(name_or_id, Some(project_name), Some(org_name))
276            .await?
277            .ok_or_else(|| {
278                anyhow::anyhow!("environment '{name_or_id}' not found in project '{project_name}'")
279            })?;
280
281        let result = self
282            .db
283            .client()
284            .query("DELETE type::record($tbl, $id)")
285            .bind(("tbl", ENV_TABLE))
286            .bind(("id", env.meta.id.clone()))
287            .await
288            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
289        result.check().map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("{e}"))?;
290        Ok(())
291    }
292}
293
294/// Row shape returned by `SELECT * FROM env`. Same conversion pattern
295/// as the sibling [`ProjectRow`](crate::project::store) / `OrgRow`:
296/// SurrealDB hands the record id back as a typed `Thing` (table + id)
297/// which serde_json renders as one of several shapes; we bridge
298/// through `serde_json::Value` and pull the inner string out in
299/// [`EnvRow::into_environment`] via
300/// [`nauka_state::sdk_bridge::thing_to_id_string`].
301#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
302struct EnvRow {
303    id: serde_json::Value,
304    name: String,
305    #[serde(default)]
306    status: Option<String>,
307    #[serde(default)]
308    labels: Option<serde_json::Value>,
309    project: String,
310    project_name: String,
311    org: String,
312    org_name: String,
313    created_at: String,
314    updated_at: String,
315}
316
317impl EnvRow {
318    fn into_environment(self) -> Environment {
319        let id = thing_to_id_string("env:", &self.id);
320        let labels = self
321            .labels
322            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v).ok())
323            .unwrap_or_default();
324        Environment {
325            meta: ResourceMeta {
326                id,
327                name: self.name,
328                status: self.status.unwrap_or_else(|| "active".to_string()),
329                labels,
330                created_at: iso8601_to_epoch(&self.created_at),
331                updated_at: iso8601_to_epoch(&self.updated_at),
332            },
333            project_id: ProjectId::from(self.project),
334            project_name: self.project_name,
335            org_id: OrgId::from(self.org),
336            org_name: self.org_name,
337        }
338    }
339}
340
341/// Deserialize the first row from a `SELECT` result set, returning
342/// `Ok(None)` when the set is empty.
343fn decode_first(raw: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Environment>> {
344    match raw.into_iter().next() {
345        None => Ok(None),
346        Some(v) => {
347            let row: EnvRow = serde_json::from_value(v)
348                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("deserialise env row: {e}"))?;
349            Ok(Some(row.into_environment()))
350        }
351    }
352}
353
354/// Wrap [`nauka_state::sdk_bridge::classify_create_error`] so the user-facing
355/// duplicate message includes the owning project. The composite
356/// unique index on `(project, name)` rejects
357/// `(proj_a, "prod")` + `(proj_a, "prod")` but allows
358/// `(proj_a, "prod")` + `(proj_b, "prod")`, so the error wording
359/// "environment 'prod' already exists in project 'web'" is both
360/// accurate and matches the pre-P2.11 wording the CLI tests expect.
361fn classify_create_error_with_project(
362    name: &str,
363    project_name: &str,
364    err_msg: &str,
365) -> anyhow::Error {
366    let lowered = err_msg.to_lowercase();
367    if lowered.contains("already contains")
368        || lowered.contains("already exists")
369        || lowered.contains("duplicate")
370    {
371        anyhow::anyhow!("environment '{name}' already exists in project '{project_name}'")
372    } else {
373        // Fall back to the shared helper for anything that isn't a
374        // duplicate — it preserves the raw error text.
375        anyhow::anyhow!(classify_create_error("environment", name, err_msg))
376    }
377}
378
379#[cfg(test)]
380mod tests {
381    use super::*;
382
383    /// Build an in-process `EnvStore` backed by a fresh SurrealKV
384    /// datastore at a temporary path, with the cluster schema bundle
385    /// applied. Returns the tempdir guard, the env store, the parent
386    /// project store, and the grand-parent org store so individual
387    /// tests can seed the hierarchy they need.
388    async fn temp_store() -> (
389        tempfile::TempDir,
390        EnvStore,
391        project::store::ProjectStore,
392        crate::store::OrgStore,
393    ) {
394        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().expect("tempdir");
395        let db = EmbeddedDb::open(&dir.path().join("envs.skv"))
396            .await
397            .expect("open EmbeddedDb at temp path");
398        nauka_state::apply_cluster_schemas(&db)
399            .await
400            .expect("apply cluster schemas");
401        let env_store = EnvStore::new(db.clone());
402        let proj_store = project::store::ProjectStore::new(db.clone());
403        let org_store = crate::store::OrgStore::new(db);
404        (dir, env_store, proj_store, org_store)
405    }
406
407    async fn seed_hierarchy(
408        proj_store: &project::store::ProjectStore,
409        org_store: &crate::store::OrgStore,
410    ) {
411        org_store.create("acme").await.expect("create org");
412        proj_store
413            .create("web", "acme")
414            .await
415            .expect("create project");
416    }
417
418    #[tokio::test]
419    async fn create_then_get_by_name() {
420        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
421        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
422
423        let env = envs
424            .create("prod", "web", "acme")
425            .await
426            .expect("create env");
427        assert_eq!(env.meta.name, "prod");
428        // `EnvId`'s prefix is `env`, not `environment`; the SurrealDB
429        // record ends up at `env:env-01J…`.
430        assert!(
431            env.meta.id.starts_with("env-"),
432            "expected env- prefix, got: {}",
433            env.meta.id
434        );
435        assert_eq!(env.project_name, "web");
436        assert_eq!(env.org_name, "acme");
437
438        let got = envs
439            .get("prod", Some("web"), Some("acme"))
440            .await
441            .expect("get by name")
442            .expect("missing");
443        assert_eq!(got.meta.id, env.meta.id);
444        assert_eq!(got.meta.name, "prod");
445    }
446
447    #[tokio::test]
448    async fn create_then_get_by_id() {
449        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
450        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
451        let env = envs
452            .create("prod", "web", "acme")
453            .await
454            .expect("create env");
455
456        let got = envs
457            .get(&env.meta.id, None, None)
458            .await
459            .expect("get by id")
460            .expect("missing");
461        assert_eq!(got.meta.id, env.meta.id);
462        assert_eq!(got.meta.name, "prod");
463    }
464
465    #[tokio::test]
466    async fn create_without_parent_project_errors() {
467        let (_d, envs, _projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
468        orgs.create("acme").await.expect("create org");
469        let err = envs
470            .create("prod", "nonexistent", "acme")
471            .await
472            .expect_err("missing project should fail");
473        assert!(
474            err.to_string().contains("not found"),
475            "expected 'not found', got: {err}"
476        );
477    }
478
479    #[tokio::test]
480    async fn duplicate_name_in_same_project_is_rejected() {
481        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
482        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
483        envs.create("prod", "web", "acme")
484            .await
485            .expect("first create");
486
487        let err = envs
488            .create("prod", "web", "acme")
489            .await
490            .expect_err("duplicate (project, name) should fail");
491        let msg = err.to_string();
492        assert!(
493            msg.contains("already exists"),
494            "expected duplicate error, got: {msg}"
495        );
496    }
497
498    #[tokio::test]
499    async fn same_name_in_different_projects_is_allowed() {
500        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
501        orgs.create("acme").await.expect("create org");
502        projs.create("web", "acme").await.expect("create acme/web");
503        projs.create("api", "acme").await.expect("create acme/api");
504
505        // Two projects within the same org may both have a `prod` env
506        // — the unique index is on `(project, name)`, not
507        // `(org, name)` or just `name`.
508        envs.create("prod", "web", "acme")
509            .await
510            .expect("create web/prod");
511        envs.create("prod", "api", "acme")
512            .await
513            .expect("create api/prod");
514
515        let web_prod = envs
516            .get("prod", Some("web"), Some("acme"))
517            .await
518            .expect("get web/prod")
519            .expect("missing");
520        let api_prod = envs
521            .get("prod", Some("api"), Some("acme"))
522            .await
523            .expect("get api/prod")
524            .expect("missing");
525        assert_ne!(web_prod.meta.id, api_prod.meta.id);
526        assert_eq!(web_prod.project_name, "web");
527        assert_eq!(api_prod.project_name, "api");
528    }
529
530    #[tokio::test]
531    async fn list_returns_all_envs() {
532        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
533        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
534        projs.create("api", "acme").await.expect("create acme/api");
535        envs.create("prod", "web", "acme").await.unwrap();
536        envs.create("staging", "web", "acme").await.unwrap();
537        envs.create("prod", "api", "acme").await.unwrap();
538
539        let all = envs.list(None, None).await.expect("list all");
540        assert_eq!(all.len(), 3);
541
542        let web_envs = envs
543            .list(Some("web"), Some("acme"))
544            .await
545            .expect("list web envs");
546        assert_eq!(web_envs.len(), 2);
547
548        let acme_envs = envs.list(None, Some("acme")).await.expect("list acme envs");
549        assert_eq!(acme_envs.len(), 3);
550    }
551
552    #[tokio::test]
553    async fn list_empty_returns_empty() {
554        let (_d, envs, _p, _o) = temp_store().await;
555        let all = envs.list(None, None).await.expect("list");
556        assert!(all.is_empty());
557    }
558
559    #[tokio::test]
560    async fn get_missing_returns_none() {
561        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
562        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
563        assert!(envs
564            .get("does-not-exist", Some("web"), Some("acme"))
565            .await
566            .unwrap()
567            .is_none());
568        // `EnvId::looks_like_id` checks for the `env-` prefix.
569        assert!(envs
570            .get("env-01J00000000000000000000000", None, None)
571            .await
572            .unwrap()
573            .is_none());
574    }
575
576    #[tokio::test]
577    async fn delete_removes_env() {
578        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
579        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
580        envs.create("prod", "web", "acme")
581            .await
582            .expect("create env");
583
584        envs.delete("prod", "web", "acme").await.expect("delete");
585        assert!(envs
586            .get("prod", Some("web"), Some("acme"))
587            .await
588            .unwrap()
589            .is_none());
590        assert!(envs.list(None, None).await.unwrap().is_empty());
591    }
592
593    #[tokio::test]
594    async fn delete_missing_env_errors() {
595        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
596        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
597        let err = envs
598            .delete("does-not-exist", "web", "acme")
599            .await
600            .expect_err("missing env should fail");
601        assert!(err.to_string().contains("not found"), "got: {err}");
602    }
603
604    /// Project delete is forbidden while a child env still exists —
605    /// mirrors the test under `ProjectStore` but now uses the real
606    /// env store to seed the child instead of a hand-crafted row.
607    #[tokio::test]
608    async fn project_delete_refuses_while_child_env_exists() {
609        let (_d, envs, projs, orgs) = temp_store().await;
610        seed_hierarchy(&projs, &orgs).await;
611        envs.create("prod", "web", "acme")
612            .await
613            .expect("create env");
614
615        let err = projs
616            .delete("web", "acme")
617            .await
618            .expect_err("project delete should refuse while an env exists");
619        let msg = err.to_string();
620        assert!(
621            msg.contains("environment") && msg.contains("Delete them first"),
622            "expected 'has N environment(s). Delete them first' message, got: {msg}",
623        );
624
625        // Removing the env unblocks the project delete.
626        envs.delete("prod", "web", "acme")
627            .await
628            .expect("delete env");
629        projs.delete("web", "acme").await.expect("delete project");
630    }
631}
